title: DDGC_newArch Framework API Drift Repair
introduction: Repair DDGC_newArch after framework API drift so cargo test compiles and runs again. Align encounter/runtime code with current framework_combat APIs, replace removed EffectNode condition fields with supported condition plumbing, stop relying on private execute_effect_node internals, and restore deterministic battle pipeline behavior covered by migration documents and regression tests.
goals: Restore `cargo check` and `cargo test` to a passing state for DDGC_newArch
Repair the DDGC conditional effect path using supported public framework APIs only
Remove reliance on private framework internals from encounter execution and scenarios
Preserve the migration's documented deterministic battle behavior where the current framework still supports it

user_stories.title: Inventory The API Drift Surface
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need an explicit inventory of compile failures and API mismatches so the repair effort is driven by the current codebase rather than stale migration ledgers.
acceptance_criteria: A checked-in repair note or progress entry lists each incompatible API assumption currently blocking compilation
The inventory groups failures by subsystem such as encounter runtime, scenarios, hero content, and test fixtures
The inventory distinguishes framework API drift from true DDGC game-layer logic gaps

user_stories.title: Realign Runtime Effect Resolution To Public Framework APIs
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need encounter execution and runtime helpers to work entirely through the current public `framework_combat` APIs so the game crate no longer depends on private helpers or obsolete return shapes.
acceptance_criteria: `src/run/encounters.rs` no longer imports private framework functions
Runtime code compiles against the current `resolve_skill` and effect-result APIs
Riposte, deferred-effect, and battle-loop plumbing compile without referencing removed `results` or `deferred` wrapper fields

user_stories.title: Reconnect DDGC Conditional Effects Through Supported Condition Plumbing
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need DDGC conditional skills to express their gating through supported condition structures or game-layer adapters so content compiles without writing removed `EffectNode` fields.
acceptance_criteria: Hero or monster content no longer assigns nonexistent `EffectNode` fields
The replacement condition path uses supported framework conditions or the existing DDGC condition adapter seam in game-layer code
Conditional skills that served as documented anchors, including the Hunter condition skills, still participate in tests or runtime scenarios in an observable way

user_stories.title: Repair Scenario And Regression Fixtures Against The Current Runtime Shape
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need regression scenarios and focused tests to match the repaired runtime interfaces so they validate current behavior instead of an outdated internal representation.
acceptance_criteria: Scenario files under `src/scenarios/` compile against the repaired runtime interfaces
Focused tests that were asserting on obsolete result wrappers are updated to supported observable outputs
Deterministic trace-oriented coverage remains in place for the repaired battle pipeline where applicable

user_stories.title: Close Out The Repair With Green Verification And Updated Planning Artifacts
user_stories.description: As a developer, I need the repair effort closed out with clean verification and updated planning artifacts so the repo state once again matches its recorded migration status.
acceptance_criteria: The resulting PRD/task state in meta-agent reflects the decomposition of this repair effort
A progress artifact records what was repaired, what remains deferred, and any residual risks tied to framework evolution

non_goals: Rewriting framework crates to reintroduce removed private APIs
Expanding DDGC migration scope beyond currently registered content
Solving deferred DDGC condition families that were already documented as future work unless required to restore compilation
